## Summary

Hyper-critical audit of the Evaluate→Revise handoff pipeline identified 6 correctness bugs across severity mapping, decision persistence, undo durability, anchor resilience, and source classification. All fixed with 39 regression tests.

## Scope

**What this touches:**
- `components/revision/ReviseWorkbenchClient.tsx` — Decision map ordering, undo persistence, selectedText audit trail, source badge
- `lib/revision/normalizeFindings.ts` — Severity mapping harmonization
- `lib/revision/proposalSynthesis.ts` — Anchor fail-closed → skip-and-continue
- `lib/revision/workbenchQueue.ts` — Source classification + sort priority
- `__tests__/lib/revision/revise-pipeline-hardening.test.ts` — 39 new regression tests

**What this does NOT touch:** Evaluation pipeline, scoring, prompt doctrine, pricing, UI layout, Track C architecture, DB schema, migrations.

### Fixes

| # | Severity | Issue | Fix |
|---|----------|-------|-----|
| 1 | **HIGH** | `rebuildDecisionMap` reversed array — earliest decision won instead of latest. Author changing their mind was silently ignored. | Iterate newest-first, first write per opportunity wins. |
| 2 | **HIGH** | `selectedText` never sent for `accepted_a/b/c` — server only knew "picked A" but not the actual proposal text at that moment. | Send `selectedProposal.text` as `selectedText` on stamp. |
| 3 | **MEDIUM** | Undo was local-only — removed entry from array but never created server-synced undo entry. Refreshing page resurrected undone decisions. | Create `isUndo: true` ledger entry with `undoneLocalId` reference, sync to server. |
| 4 | **MEDIUM** | `normalizeFindings.toSeverity()` didn't recognize `must`, `could`, `blocker`, `optional` — only `high`/`critical`/`major`/`low`/`minor`. `deepRevisionExtraction.toSeverity()` had the broader mapping. Findings using user-facing severity labels defaulted to `medium` (SHOULD). | Harmonized to match: `must/blocker` → high, `could/optional` → low. |
| 5 | **LOW** | `proposalSynthesis.toProposalInputs` threw on first anchor failure, blocking entire proposal set. One bad finding prevented all repairs. | Skip + warn on anchor failure, continue generating remaining proposals. |
| 6 | **LOW** | No way to distinguish evaluation-derived findings from baseline discovery noise in the workbench queue. | Added `source: evaluation \| deep_revision \| baseline_discovery` field, sort by source within severity tier, display source badge in UI. |

## Evaluation Process Change Declaration

- [x] This PR does NOT change the evaluation pipeline behavior
- [x] This PR does NOT change scoring, prompt doctrine, or pricing
- [x] This PR changes the **revision** pipeline (Revise workbench, severity mapping, proposal synthesis)

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- **rebuildDecisionMap fix** — Changes behavior for users who changed their mind on a decision. Previously the first decision stuck; now the latest decision wins. This is the correct behavior.
- **Undo creates a new entry** — Instead of removing the entry, undo now adds an `isUndo: true` entry. This is auditable and server-persistent but increases ledger size. The `rebuildDecisionMap` correctly handles this since the undo entry (newest) wins.
- **Anchor skip-and-continue** — Previously a single bad anchor blocked all proposals. Now it's logged as a warning and skipped. The finding still exists but won't get an automated proposal — author must revise manually.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — no changes to evaluation pipeline, phase transitions, or worker routes.
